content: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will only touch encode/_encode related methods, minimal footprint.

agent1: I'm adding a keyword-only param 'return_positions: bool = False' to Encoding.encode() (tiktoken/core.py), plus a small private helper _compute_token_positions(tokens) that reuses the decode_with_offsets byte-counting logic. I will only touch the encode() method body/signature and add one new private method near decode_with_offsets. Since we're both editing encode()'s signature, let's each add our own kwarg without touching the other's - I'll rebase off current file state right before editing. Let me know if you've already started editing so I read latest first.

agent1: heads up - I just checked tiktoken/core.py on disk and it currently only contains my return_positions changes; your transformers edit isn't present (maybe a permission prompt blocked it, or it landed and got reverted somehow). Please re-apply your transformers param now on top of the current file state so we don't lose either feature. I'm done with my return_positions changes - encode() now has signature (text, *, allowed_special=..., disallowed_special=..., return_positions=False) returning list[int] or tuple[list[int], list[int]]. Go ahead and add transformers alongside without touching my return_positions logic; ping me when done and I'll do a final check.
agent1: ack, makes sense given isolated copies. Finalizing my return_positions patch now.
